description: Documented behavioral differences between CoPaw domain (task.py / hooks) and TeamHarness MCP (server.py). Phase 0 records current state; Phase 5 resolves via shared protocol + adapters.

diffs.summary: TeamHarness MCP exposes project/task actions not present on CoPaw hooks
diffs.copaw_hooks: delegate_task, check_task, ack_task, submit_task; projectflow: create_project, plan_dag, plan_loop, ready_*, pause/resume/complete, record_loop_iteration, check_active_tasks
diffs.teamharness_mcp: Additional: create_quick_project, resolve_project, accept_task_result, mark_requester_report_sent, cancel_task
diffs.resolution_phase: P5.3 — shared core + thin MCP adapter keeps JSON shape

diffs.summary: MCP enforces runtime role on taskflow actions; CoPaw hooks use Matrix actor + Team Room checks
diffs.copaw_hooks: taskflow._require_team_leader_assignment_room for delegate; worker identity from AGENTTEAMS_MATRIX_USER_ID
diffs.teamharness_mcp: _role / _runtime_role gates leader vs worker vs remote-member per action
diffs.resolution_phase: P5.2/P5.3 — policy in adapter, domain stays role-agnostic

diffs.summary: CoPaw hooks push/pull MinIO around ack/submit/delegate; MCP uses _filesync mc subprocess
diffs.copaw_hooks: create_sync() from filesync.py; push excludes spec.md and base/
diffs.resolution_phase: P5.7 — MCP filesync delegates to agentteams_sync.FileSync with explicit storage prefixes; global-shared push remains read-only

diffs.summary: MCP submit_task may publish artifacts to Matrix; CoPaw domain writes result.md only
DAG node completion in MCP uses accept_task_result; CoPaw projectflow uses plan marker updates via separate flows
diffs.copaw_domain: check_task reads result; plan completion markers updated outside submit_task in Leader workflows
diffs.teamharness_mcp: accept_task_result mutates project plan node status and may publish project result
